name: Relativity E-Discovery & Legal Review API
what_it_does: Relativity REST API for e-discovery and legal document review platform. Enables AI agents to manage document review workspaces, handle document ingestion and processing pipelines, access review and coding decisions on documents, retrieve privilege log and responsiveness data, manage search and analytics queries, access production and export workflows, retrieve reviewer assignment and progress data, handle legal hold management, and integrate e-discovery data with case management and litigation support systems.
use_cases: Managing document review workspaces and workflows from litigation support agents
Accessing privilege and responsiveness coding decisions from legal analytics agents
Retrieving production set and export data from document production agents
Managing legal holds and custodian data from litigation hold management agents
not_for: General document management without e-discovery and legal review workflows
Small law firms without large-scale document review needs
Non-litigation compliance without formal e-discovery requirements
best_when: A law firm, corporate legal department, or e-discovery service provider using Relativity wants AI agents to automate document review workflows, privilege logging, production management, and litigation analytics.
avoid_when: PRIVACY+PRIVILEGE: E-discovery documents contain highly sensitive information including privileged attorney-client communications, work product, and confidential business data under legal hold. Automated privilege decisions must have attorney oversight. Inadvertent privilege waiver is a legal risk.

auth.notes: Relativity uses OAuth 2.0 and API key for API access. Workspace-level and system-level credentials. RelativityOne (cloud) and Relativity (on-premises) have different API configurations. Relativity Developer Portal with C# and Python SDKs. Strong developer ecosystem.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — e-discovery enterprise pricing
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-GB storage and per-user review pricing; RelativityOne subscription
pricing.notes: Chicago, Illinois. Founded 2001. Market leader for e-discovery software globally. RelativityOne is the cloud version. Used by 180+ of the AmLaw 200. 10,000+ law firms and corporations. Acquired kCura 2001, rebranded Relativity 2016. Strong AI/ML features (Active Learning). Competes with Nuix and Reveal.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API client; enterprise SLA-based

known_agent_gotchas: PRIVACY+PRIVILEGE: E-discovery docs include privileged communications — automated privilege decisions need attorney oversight
No public MCP server — OAuth2 REST API with C# and Python SDKs
Inadvertent privilege waiver risk — automated privilege coding without attorney review is legally risky
RelativityOne (cloud) vs on-premises have different API endpoints — verify deployment
Legal hold data has specific preservation requirements — deletion automation must check hold status
Active Learning AI review features are separate from API — RAL model configuration not fully API-controllable
agent_readiness.security_notes: E-discovery legal data with privilege. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR, FedRAMP. OAuth2 + API key. Multi-region. Attorney-client privilege. Legal hold compliance.

security_score_components.security_notes: E-discovery legal data. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR, FedRAMP. OAuth2. Multi-region. Privilege protections. Legal hold.
